note: task-implement is the EXECUTE phase of the taskmaster pipeline: it takes a spec that already passed the Definition-of-Ready gate and implements it interactively in the current session (Chosen Approach for features, regression-test-first for bugfixes), then auto-chains into task-verify and task-deliver. Near-misses belong to the authoring phase (task-start), the standalone gate run (task-verify), the commit+PR phase (task-deliver), PR-feedback (task-fix-pr), behaviour-preserving refactor execution (refactor-apply), or plain debug/explain. mock_rate encodes the ideal outcome for --decider mock; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: implement this spec
run the task — the spec is ready, go build it
execute .marvin/task/014-oauth-refresh.md in this session
task-start gave me the spec, now actually build it out following the chosen approach
the ready spec for the rate-limiter is written, implement it and write the tests for each acceptance criterion
work through the bugfix spec — regression test first, then the fix, don't dispatch to a worktree
marvin implement the spec, keep me in the loop for each step
carry out the spec by slug: payment-retry, stay on the current branch
we defined the task earlier — go ahead and code it up against the file allowlist
the DoR gate passed, execute the spec interactively and self-test it before handing off
spec out the new webhook feature — gather context and produce an immutable spec
queries.note: authoring the spec, not executing one → task-start

queries.text: run the quality gates — tests, lint, type-check, build — and tell me if it's green
commit what I built and open the PR for it
apply the reviewer comments on my PR and reply to each thread
queries.note: turning PR feedback into commits → task-fix-pr

queries.text: execute step 2 of the refactoring plan, behaviour-preserving, keep tests green
queries.note: behaviour-preserving refactor step, not a spec feature/fix → refactor-apply

queries.text: the spec is still a draft — help me finish authoring it and pass the DoR gate
queries.note: draft not ready; finishing authoring → task-start

queries.text: my implementation broke three tests, help me figure out the root cause
explain how the retry pipeline in this module works before I touch it
just add a null check on line 42 of src/parser.ts
queries.note: trivial one-line edit, no spec pipeline → plain edit

queries.text: dispatch this spec to a headless worktree agent instead of doing it here
queries.note: hands-off worktree dispatch is dispatch.sh / marvin-tm-executor, not the interactive skill

queries.text: the spec passed DoR — now actually implement it and run the gates for me
queries.note: mentions gates but the primary verb is implement; verify is auto-chained downstream → beats task-verify

queries.text: build out the ready spec and take it all the way through to the PR
queries.note: names the PR but the entry point is implementation, which auto-chains into deliver → beats task-deliver

queries.text: spec this out first — I need an immutable, testable spec with acceptance criteria before any code
queries.note: authoring precedes execution; no spec exists yet → must lose to task-start

queries.text: implementation's done and verified — commit it and open the pull request
queries.note: post-implementation delivery; work already built and green → must lose to task-deliver
